_comment: EXAMPLE FILE — copy to ~/.config/coursers/course-correct-rules.json to activate. This is the author's working ruleset; edit to suit your workflow. See README for schema docs.

rules.description: Use the Grep tool instead of grep/rg in Bash
rules.message: Use the Grep tool instead. Example: Grep(pattern='<your pattern>', path='<dir or file>'). Pipelines (`| grep`) are allowed and auto-rewritten to rg.

rules._todo: stub: block 'sed -n <range>p file' pattern and redirect to Read tool with offset/limit. Enable once Read offset/limit parameter syntax is confirmed stable (currently broken with string-typed params in this environment).
rules.description: Use the Read tool instead of sed -n for line-range reads
rules.message: Use Read(file_path='<path>', offset=N, limit=N) instead of sed -n for line-range reads.

rules.description: Use the Read tool instead of cat for reading files
rules.message: Use the Read tool. Example: Read(file_path='<path>'). Heredocs and pipelines are allowed.

rules.description: Use the Read tool with offset/limit instead of head/tail
rules.message: Use Read(file_path='<path>', offset=N, limit=N) or `tail` in pipelines. `tail -f` is allowed.

rules.description: Use the Glob tool instead of find for file discovery
rules.message: Use the Glob tool. Example: Glob(pattern='**/*.rs', path='<dir>'). Use find only for -exec/-delete/-mtime.
Use `bun`/`bunx`. Examples: `bun install`, `bun run build`, `bunx tsc`.

rules.description: Use mise instead of nvm for Node.js version management
Use uv instead of pip/pip3 for package management
Do not sleep — find productive work or use run_in_background
rules.message: Do not sleep. If waiting for a background task, use `run_in_background` and you will be notified when it completes. If polling, run the check command directly (e.g. `gh run view`). If blocked, find other work to do in the meantime.

rules.description: Use the Glob tool instead of ls for directory listing
rules._todo: comment-false-positive: add '^\\s*#' exception to stop comment lines like '# ls foo' from firing this rule. Same fix needed for no-cat-use-read. Implement once confirmed the rules engine matches full command string including leading whitespace.
rules.message: Use the Glob tool for file discovery. Example: Glob(pattern='*', path='<dir>') or Glob(pattern='**/*.rs'). Glob returns sorted results with no token overhead from formatting.

rules.description: Use the Edit tool instead of sed for in-place file edits
rules.message: Use the Edit tool for file modifications. Example: Edit(file_path='<path>', old_string='...', new_string='...'). Edit provides better diffing, review, and undo. Piped sed for text transformation is fine.

rules.description: All Bash tool calls must use nu -c — wrap every command in nu
rules.message: Wrap this in `nu -c "..."`. All Bash tool calls must use Nushell. Example: Bash(nu -c "<command>").

rules.description: Do not cd — use absolute paths or git -C instead
rules.message: Do not change directories. Use absolute paths in commands, `git -C <path>` for git operations, or pass `path` parameters to tools (Glob, Grep, Read). Changing directory loses working directory state and breaks hooks that depend on cwd.

failure_learning.message_template:
[course-correct] This exact command has failed {count} times in the last {window} minutes. Consider a different approach — check the path/syntax, use a dedicated tool, or break the command into smaller steps.

Failing command: {preview}
